decisions.title: Rust owns normal coding-agent runtime execution
decisions.summary: Normal Spark chat, agent-turn, and codergen-adjacent agent execution must enter crates/spark-agent-adapter and must not rely on Python src/agent modules as the runtime implementation.
decisions.behavioral_contract: A normal Spark agent-backed turn is processed by a Rust-owned session loop with Rust-owned tool execution and typed events. Python src/agent may remain importable for compatibility tests, oracle comparison, and historical reference, but it is not the normal distributed runtime path.
decisions.implementation_contract: Implement the session loop, provider profiles, tools, environment, subagents, and Spark adapter wiring in crates/spark-agent-adapter and Rust consumers. Do not satisfy the runtime boundary by importing Python agent.Session, Python provider profiles, Python tool execution, or Python local environment objects from normal Spark execution paths.
decisions.validation_expectations: Behavior tests must prove chat, agent-turn, and codergen-adjacent paths produce observable Rust-backed outputs through public APIs. Documentation must identify retained Python src/agent status. uv run pytest -q remains the completion gate.

decisions.title: The agent loop uses low-level unified LLM calls
decisions.summary: The Rust agent loop builds unified LLM Requests and calls Client.complete or Client.stream directly instead of using a high-level SDK tool loop.
decisions.behavioral_contract: The agent controls every tool round, steering injection, truncation step, loop detection check, event emission, timeout, abort, and shutdown. LLM provider communication is delegated only to crates/unified-llm-adapter low-level complete or stream calls.
decisions.implementation_contract: Construct Request values with provider profile system prompt, converted history, active tool definitions, tool_choice auto, provider id, provider_options, and current reasoning_effort. Do not call unified_llm_adapter high-level generate for the agent loop and do not call Python unified_llm clients.
decisions.validation_expectations: Fake ProviderAdapter or Client tests must inspect low-level requests, tool definitions, tool_choice, provider metadata, and reasoning_effort across multiple tool rounds and streaming conversions.

decisions.title: Session events include stream-derived extensions
decisions.summary: The session event API includes all Section 2.9 lifecycle events plus reasoning, model-proposed tool-call, and usage events from Section 1.6.
decisions.behavioral_contract: Hosts can distinguish assistant text, assistant reasoning, model-proposed tool calls, actual tool execution, usage updates, warnings, errors, limits, steering, processing completion, and session start/end. Model-proposed tool-call events do not imply the tool has executed.
decisions.implementation_contract: Expose idiomatic Rust enum variants and stable serialized event kind strings. Add ASSISTANT_REASONING_START, ASSISTANT_REASONING_DELTA, ASSISTANT_REASONING_END, MODEL_TOOL_CALL_START, MODEL_TOOL_CALL_DELTA, MODEL_TOOL_CALL_END, and MODEL_USAGE_UPDATE alongside the Section 2.9 event set. Map these events into spark_common::events::TurnStreamEvent without changing existing Workspace payload semantics.
decisions.validation_expectations: Streaming tests must assert event ordering, distinct model-tool versus execution-tool events, usage updates, lifecycle bracketing, and Spark TurnStreamEvent mapping through public event streams.

decisions.title: Provider profiles are provider-native with Spark selector compatibility
decisions.summary: OpenAI, Anthropic, and Gemini profiles preserve provider-native model-facing tools and prompts, while Spark compatibility selectors are explicit extensions rather than a universal profile.
decisions.behavioral_contract: OpenAI exposes codex-aligned apply_patch as the primary edit surface, Anthropic exposes old_string/new_string edit_file and not apply_patch as its native edit path, and Gemini exposes gemini-cli-style file and directory tools. Spark selectors such as codex, openrouter, litellm, openai_compatible, and configured llm profiles are normalized at the adapter boundary without erasing the native profile contract.
decisions.implementation_contract: Implement ProviderProfile for native openai, anthropic, and gemini. Treat provider=codex as the first-class Rust Codex app-server runtime path, not as openai_compatible and not as a reason to invoke Python agent code. OpenAI-compatible selectors may use an explicit compatibility profile backed by unified-llm-adapter, but they do not close native provider parity requirements.
decisions.validation_expectations: Profile tests must assert observable tool names, editing formats, timeout defaults, provider_options, custom latest-wins registration, and selector normalization without asserting byte-for-byte prompt strings.

decisions.title: Tool dispatch is Rust-owned and environment-mediated
decisions.summary: Tool lookup, JSON Schema validation, built-in tool execution, truncation, event emission, and ToolResult construction happen inside the Rust agent runtime through ExecutionEnvironment.
decisions.behavioral_contract: Unknown tools, invalid arguments, and executor failures are recoverable error ToolResult values visible to the model. Built-in file, patch, shell, grep, glob, list, and subagent tools run through the active execution environment and emit execution events.
decisions.implementation_contract: Implement ToolRegistry and dispatch in crates/spark-agent-adapter. Use jsonschema validation for tool parameters, Rust-owned built-in executors, and the ExecutionEnvironment trait for side effects. Do not call Python builtin_tools, Python apply_patch, or direct filesystem/process helpers from tool implementations except through LocalExecutionEnvironment.
decisions.validation_expectations: Tests must cover lookup order, latest-wins overrides, schema validation, invalid JSON, unknown tools, execution failures, parallel calls, result association by tool_call_id, v4a apply_patch behavior, and event emission.

decisions.title: Local execution policy is part of the public runtime contract
decisions.summary: LocalExecutionEnvironment defines path resolution, command spawning, timeout cleanup, environment filtering, grep fallback, and profile-specific shell defaults.
decisions.behavioral_contract: Local tools resolve paths relative to working_directory, commands run in a killable process group through the platform shell, timeouts collect partial output, and sensitive environment variables are excluded by default. Anthropic shell calls default to 120 seconds unless timeout_ms is supplied.
decisions.implementation_contract: Track running command process groups for timeout and abort cleanup. Enforce default_command_timeout_ms, max_command_timeout_ms, profile-specific shell defaults, SIGTERM plus 2 second grace plus SIGKILL on Unix, and an equivalent Windows cleanup strategy. Implement inherit-all, inherit-none, and core-safe environment policies.
decisions.validation_expectations: Behavior tests must run against temporary directories and controlled environment maps, covering relative paths, parent directory creation, command output fields, timeout messages, process cleanup, env filtering, ripgrep use or native fallback, and custom environment trait implementation.

decisions.title: Full tool output belongs to events and truncated output belongs to the LLM
decisions.summary: Tool output is character-truncated first and line-truncated second before ToolResult construction, while TOOL_CALL_END carries the full untruncated output.
decisions.behavioral_contract: The model is explicitly warned whenever it receives incomplete tool output. Hosts and logs can still inspect the complete output from the event stream.
decisions.implementation_contract: Apply per-tool default and SessionConfig override character limits before line limits. Preserve valid UTF-8 character boundaries and insert the spec warning markers with removed character or line counts. Emit TOOL_CALL_END with raw output or error before returning the truncated ToolResult content to the loop.
decisions.validation_expectations: Tests must cover head_tail and tail modes, pathological long-line output, line truncation after character truncation, override limits, marker text, and full-output availability in events.

decisions.title: Prompt context is layered behavior, not prompt string parity
decisions.summary: Provider prompts are built from required layers and project documents, but validation targets prompt behavior and included sections rather than exact source strings.
decisions.behavioral_contract: The model sees provider-specific base guidance, structured environment context, tool descriptions, project instructions, and user overrides in the required precedence order. Context usage warnings are informational and do not trigger automatic compaction.
decisions.implementation_contract: Snapshot environment and git context at session start, discover AGENTS.md plus active-provider instruction files from root to working directory, enforce a 32 KB total project instruction budget with the required marker, and convert SteeringTurn values to user-role messages.
decisions.validation_expectations: Tests must assert included prompt layers, provider-specific project file selection, root-before-deeper ordering, truncation marker behavior, git/environment fields, context warning threshold, and absence of brittle full-prompt string assertions.

decisions.title: Steering, follow-ups, and reasoning changes are next-call controls
decisions.summary: Host controls are queued and applied at deterministic loop boundaries without restarting the session.
decisions.behavioral_contract: Steering messages inject before the next LLM call after a tool round, idle steering is retained until the next submit, follow-ups start after natural completion, and reasoning_effort changes affect the next low-level request.
decisions.implementation_contract: Store steering and follow-up queues on Session behind the same synchronization used by submit and close. Expose Spark child-intervention hooks as steering where a turn is active. Do not implement steering by restarting or replacing session history.
decisions.validation_expectations: Tests must cover idle steering, mid-tool steering, follow-up sequencing, history preservation, event emission, request role conversion, and reasoning_effort updates between model calls.

decisions.title: Subagents are child sessions sharing execution environment
decisions.summary: Subagent tools create independent child Session values that share the parent ExecutionEnvironment and are depth-limited by default.
decisions.behavioral_contract: A parent can spawn, message, wait for, and close scoped child agents. Child histories are independent, filesystem effects are shared through the environment, depth limits prevent recursive spawning by default, and parent shutdown closes children.
decisions.implementation_contract: Register spawn_agent, send_input, wait, and close_agent as profile tools. Maintain a subagent registry on Session, use parent profile unless an allowed model override is supplied, apply child max_turns, and surface subagent progress and results in the parent event stream.
decisions.validation_expectations: Tests must cover spawn arguments, working_dir scoping, independent history, shared filesystem effects, model override policy, depth rejection, wait result shape, close behavior, parent abort cleanup, and parent ToolResult output.

decisions.title: Spark adapter payloads remain compatibility contracts
decisions.summary: AgentTurnOutput, TurnStreamEvent mapping, token usage, raw logs, and thread resume failure payloads are preserved while the backing runtime changes to Rust.
decisions.behavioral_contract: Workspace and API callers continue to observe the same conversation lifecycle, final assistant text, streaming segments, tool records, token usage shape, raw log lines, request-user-input records, thread resume failure details, and error reporting shapes.
decisions.implementation_contract: Keep AgentTurnRequest and AgentTurnOutput as the Spark-facing adapter boundary over Session events. Map session events into spark_common TurnStreamEvent values before Workspace ingestion. Preserve provider, model, llm_profile, reasoning_effort, project_path, conversation_id, chat_mode, and metadata across the Rust boundary.
decisions.validation_expectations: Compatibility tests must exercise HTTP routes, Workspace services, and Python facades through real public APIs and persisted snapshots rather than checking imports or source text.

decisions.title: Error handling separates recoverable tool data from session closure
decisions.summary: Tool failures are returned to the model, while aborts and unrecoverable provider failures run graceful shutdown and close the session.
decisions.behavioral_contract: The model can recover from tool-level failures. Authentication errors, aborts, and unrecoverable session errors emit structured ERROR and SESSION_END events and transition to CLOSED. Turn limits return to IDLE instead of closing.
decisions.implementation_contract: Map unified LLM adapter errors into session-level categories, catch tool errors at dispatch, track command process groups and subagents for cleanup, and emit enough structured error data for Workspace persistence and thread-resume compatibility.
decisions.validation_expectations: Tests must cover unknown tools, invalid args, file errors, shell nonzero, shell timeout, transient provider retry delegation, auth failure close, context length warning, turn limit IDLE behavior, abort cleanup, event flushing, and subagent shutdown.

decisions.title: Validation is behavioral, cross-provider, and documentation-backed
decisions.summary: Completion evidence must demonstrate Rust-owned behavior through public interfaces and documents, with Python tests retained only as oracle or compatibility support.
decisions.behavioral_contract: The runtime is complete only when observable session, provider, tool, environment, event, prompt, steering, subagent, error, Spark integration, and documentation behavior is covered. Live provider smoke tests are optional and skipped without credentials.
decisions.implementation_contract: Add or update Rust crate tests, Workspace/API tests, and tests/compat/agent as behavior tests. Keep tests independent of source wording, prompt byte strings, runtime alias paths, and deprecated details. Update docs/rust-rewrite-migration.md and README.md before final distribution-readiness claims.
decisions.validation_expectations: Milestones may run targeted cargo tests as supplemental evidence, but before reporting a code-change milestone complete the repository must pass uv run pytest -q unless the user explicitly changes the policy.
